perturbation.original_text: Each party understands and acknowledges that neither party nor any of its Representatives makes any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material made available by it or to it.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party understands and acknowledges that the disclosing party warrants that the Evaluation Material is accurate and complete to the best of its knowledge, but neither party nor any of its Representatives makes any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material made available by it or to it.
perturbation.explanation: The added phrase 'the disclosing party warrants that the Evaluation Material is accurate and complete to the best of its knowledge' contradicts the original statement disclaiming any representation or warranty regarding the accuracy of the Evaluation Material. This in-text contradiction introduces uncertainty about the liability of the disclosing party regarding the information provided.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Representation of Accuracy . Each party understands and acknowledges that the disclosing party warrants that the Evaluation Material is accurate and complete to the best of its knowledge, but neither party nor any of its Representatives makes any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material made available by it or to it. Each party agrees that neither party nor any of its Representatives shall have any liability to the other party or to any of its Representatives relating to or resulting from the use of or reliance upon such other party’s Evaluation Material or any errors therein or omissions therefrom. Only those representations or warranties which are made in a final definitive agreement regarding the Transaction, when, as and if executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified therein, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a warranty that the Evaluation Material is accurate to the best of the disclosing party's knowledge. This directly contradicts the subsequent statement in both the Changed Text and Contradicted Text, which disclaims any representation or warranty regarding the accuracy of the Evaluation Material. The added warranty creates a clear and direct conflict, making the clauses incompatible because one asserts accuracy (with a knowledge qualifier), while the other denies any warranty of accuracy.

perturbation.original_text: In consideration of the Evaluation Material being furnished to you, each party agrees that for a period of one year from the date set forth above, without the other party’s prior written consent, neither party nor its affiliates will solicit, directly or indirectly, to employ any person who is now employed by the other party or its affiliates and is directly involved in the discussions hereunder (a “Covered Person”).
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of the Evaluation Material being furnished to you, each party agrees that for a period of six months from the date set forth above, without the other party’s prior written consent, neither party nor its affiliates will solicit, directly or indirectly, to employ any person who is now employed by the other party or its affiliates and is directly involved in the discussions hereunder (a “Covered Person”). However, after three months from the date set forth above, with the other party's consent which shall not be unreasonably withheld, the mentioned restriction will not apply.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces a contradiction by initially setting a non-solicitation period of six months, but then conditionally allowing solicitation after three months with consent. The original text sets a clear one-year restriction, but after the change the term is cut in half and potentially cut again in half if the other party provides consent after three months. This in-text contradiction creates ambiguity about the actual duration of the non-solicitation agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Solicitation . In consideration of the Evaluation Material being furnished to you, each party agrees that for a period of six months from the date set forth above, without the other party’s prior written consent, neither party nor its affiliates will solicit, directly or indirectly, to employ any person who is now employed by the other party or its affiliates and is directly involved in the discussions hereunder (a “Covered Person”). However, after three months from the date set forth above, with the other party's consent which shall not be unreasonably withheld, the mentioned restriction will not apply. The term “solicit to employ” shall not be deemed to include: (i) general solicitations of employment not specifically directed toward employees of the other party, (ii) an event in which such Covered Person contacts the such party or its Representatives on the Covered Person’s own initiative without any direct or indirect solicitation by or encouragement from such party or its Representatives, or (iii) such Covered Person is referred to such party by search firms, employment agencies, or other similar entities, provided that such entities have not been specifically instructed by such party to solicit the employees of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the non-solicitation period from a fixed one year in the original text to a conditional six months, with the possibility of it being reduced to three months with consent. The contradicted text includes the modified six month/three month with consent clause. The change introduces an in-text contradiction because the original fixed one-year term is incompatible with the revised conditional six-month term, as per the explanation provided and the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'. Both sections refer to the same obligation (non-solicitation), but the original text provided for a one year restriction period while the changed text specifies a six month restriction period, which can be further reduced. This constitutes a strong contradiction. Furthermore, the contradiction definition is met because this introduces conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The disclosing party acknowledges that the receiving party may currently be, or may in the future become, a competitor of the disclosing party or may be engaged or in discussions with competitors of the disclosing party. The terms of this letter agreement shall not be construed to limit the receiving party’s right to develop independently or acquire products without use of the Evaluation Material.
perturbation.changed_text: The disclosing party acknowledges that the receiving party may currently be, or may in the future become, a competitor of the disclosing party or may be engaged or in discussions with competitors of the disclosing party. The terms of this letter agreement shall be construed to limit the receiving party’s right to develop independently or acquire products similar to the Evaluation Material, even without direct use of the Evaluation Material itself, for a period of 6 months from the effective date.
perturbation.explanation: The text was changed from 'shall not be construed to limit' to 'shall be construed to limit'. This is an in-text contradiction because the original text explicitly allows the receiving party to develop independently, but the modified text restricts that right. This contradicts the receiving party's right to independent development and creates legal ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Independent Development. The disclosing party acknowledges that the receiving party may currently be, or may in the future become, a competitor of the disclosing party or may be engaged or in discussions with competitors of the disclosing party. The terms of this letter agreement shall be construed to limit the receiving party’s right to develop independently or acquire products similar to the Evaluation Material, even without direct use of the Evaluation Material itself, for a period of 6 months from the effective date. The disclosing party acknowledges that receiving party may currently or in the future be developing information internally, or be receiving information from other parties, that may be similar or related to the Evaluation Material. Accordingly, nothing in this letter agreement shall be construed as a representation or inference that receiving party does not have or shall not develop, or have developed for it or for any third party, or engage any third party that has rights to, products, concepts, systems or techniques, that, without violation of this letter agreement, are similar to or compete with the products, concepts, systems or techniques contemplated by or embodied in the Evaluation Material.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text from explicitly stating that the agreement 'shall not be construed to limit' the receiving party's right to independent development to 'shall be construed to limit' that right. The contradicted text includes the changed text, meaning the changed text is in the same location as the contradicted text. This direct reversal creates a clear contradiction within the document regarding the receiving party's ability to develop similar products independently, fitting the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: Each party is aware, and will advise its Representatives who are informed of the matters that are the subject of this Agreement, of the restrictions imposed by Federal and state securities laws on the purchase or sale of securities by it or its Representatives have received material, nonpublic information
perturbation.changed_text: Each party is aware, and will advise its Representatives who are informed of the matters that are the subject of this Agreement, that they are encouraged, but not required, to consider the restrictions imposed by Federal and state securities laws on the purchase or sale of securities by it or its Representatives have received material, nonpublic information
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'will advise of the restrictions' to 'are encouraged, but not required, to consider the restrictions' introduces a contradiction by weakening the obligation to inform representatives about securities law restrictions. This contradicts the purpose of the original clause, which sought to ensure compliance with securities laws. It makes it uncertain to whether the representatives should consider the restriction or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Securities Laws . Each party is aware, and will advise its Representatives who are informed of the matters that are the subject of this Agreement, that they are encouraged, but not required, to consider the restrictions imposed by Federal and state securities laws on the purchase or sale of securities by it or its Representatives have received material, nonpublic information (i.e., information that is (i) specific, (ii) non- public and (iii) likely to have a material effect on the market price of the issuer’s securities if and when made public (such that a reasonable investor would consider the information material in deciding whether to buy, hold or sell the issuer’s securities)) from the issuer of such securities and on the communication of such information to any other person or entity when it is reasonably foreseeable that such other person or entity is likely to purchase or sell securities.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that each party 'will advise' its representatives of the restrictions imposed by securities laws. The changed text weakens this obligation to 'are encouraged, but not required, to consider the restrictions'. This creates a direct conflict, as the original text mandates advising representatives, while the changed text only encourages consideration, leading to uncertainty and potential non-compliance. This contradicts the purpose of the original clause, which sought to ensure compliance with securities laws, thereby introducing an inconsistency as per the Perturbation Type definition. The Contradicted text is the same as the Changed Text, this happens when the change to the text directly contradicts itself.

perturbation.original_text: You agree that, for the period ending the earlier of (i) one year from the effective date of this Agreement and (ii) the date the Company enters into a definitive agreement with another party with respect to a Transaction, you will not, and you will not authorize or permit any Representatives acting on your behalf to, without the prior approval of the Board of Directors of the Company, (a) acquire or make any proposal to acquire any securities or assets of the Company
perturbation.changed_text: You agree that, for the period ending the earlier of (i) one year from the effective date of this Agreement and (ii) the date the Company enters into a definitive agreement with another party with respect to a Transaction, you may, and you are authorized to permit any Representatives acting on your behalf to, without the prior approval of the Board of Directors of the Company, (a) acquire or make any proposal to acquire any securities or assets of the Company
perturbation.explanation: The insertion of 'may' and 'are authorized' contradicts the explicit 'will not' restriction to acquire any securities or assets of the Company. The in-text contradiction introduces uncertainty about the standstill period and whether the acquiring party is actually restricted from acquiring any securities or assets of the company.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Standstill . You agree that, for the period ending the earlier of (i) one year from the effective date of this Agreement and (ii) the date the Company enters into a definitive agreement with another party with respect to a Transaction, you may, and you are authorized to permit any Representatives acting on your behalf to, without the prior approval of the Board of Directors of the Company, (a) acquire or make any proposal to acquire any securities or assets of the Company, (b) propose to enter into any merger, consolidation, business combination, restructuring, recapitalization or other extraordinary transaction of or involving Company or the purchase of a material portion of the assets of Company, (c) make or participate in any solicitation of proxies to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of any securities of Company, (d) otherwise act or seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors, or policies of Company, (e) form, join, or participate in a “group” (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended) in connection with any of the foregoing, (f) disclose any intention, plan, or arrangement inconsistent with any of the foregoing, or (g) take any action which might require Company to make a public announcement regarding the possibility of the Transaction. Notwithstanding anything contained herein to the contrary, you are permitted to purchase equity securities in the ordinary course of business that does not in any event result in an aggregate ownership by you of more than 10% of the outstanding amount of any class of equity securities of Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states 'you will not' acquire securities, while the changed text states 'you may' acquire securities. This directly contradicts the standstill provision, creating a strong in-text contradiction as it introduces conflicting obligations regarding the acquisition of securities, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation. The Contradicted Text contains both the original and changed texts, further highlighting the direct contradiction.
